verdict_reasoning: SRC is a non-receptor tyrosine kinase anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation. Its catalytic and SH2/SH3 domains face the cytosol, signaling downstream of surface receptors. No extracellular exposure at any lifecycle stage. Walking contextual buckets: cell_state_induced—no activation-induced flipping to outer leaflet documented; tissue_restricted_surface—ubiquitous cytoplasmic kinase, no lineage with outer-leaflet display; lysosomal_exocytosis—not a lysosomal TM protein; dual_localization—endosomal/perinuclear pools exist but all cytoplasmic-facing; stable_surface_attachment—not secreted. All therapeutic programs (dasatinib, bosutinib, saracatinib) are cell-penetrant small-molecule kinase inhibitors targeting the intracellular ATP pocket.
